ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn class for raisin bread with peanut butter; mapped to parent bread food product class as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no specific code for raisin bread with peanut butter topping. Mapped to category level as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this composite bread-spread food item.

serving.description: 1 slice raisin bread with peanut butter (~40g bread + ~15g peanut butter)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 55g, consisting of one slice of raisin bread (about 40g) spread with approximately 15g of peanut butter.

nutrient_source.title: Raisin bread with peanut butter — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database (HPB)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for raisin bread with peanut butter (cr_id F05020099). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite (raisin bread + peanut butter) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — white bread and raisin bread analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for raisin bread with peanut butter as a composite. GI of 70 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entry for raisin bread (GI ~63–70, mean ~70 for white-flour-based raisin bread). Peanut butter addition lowers effective glycaemic response but the bread base dominates; GI conservatively retained at 70 for the composite. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × available carbohydrate per serving (47.91 g/100g × 55g serving = 26.35g available carbs) / 100 = 18.4; adjusted to 16.5 accounting for fibre offset (net carbs ~23.6g × 70/100 = 16.5). Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 334mg/100g is in the borderline-high range; potassium at 70mg/100g provides limited offset to the sodium load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice (~55g serving, ~184mg sodium) and choose natural peanut butter with no added salt to reduce total sodium intake; pairing with potassium-rich foods such as banana or low-fat yogurt can help offset the sodium-to-potassium imbalance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (47.9g/100g) with an estimated GI of 70 creates a significant glycaemic load; peanut butter protein and fat partially blunt the glycaemic response but the refined raisin bread base remains the dominant driver.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one thin slice (~40g bread), use natural peanut butter without added sugar, choose wholemeal raisin bread to lower GI and increase fibre, and pair with a protein source to further attenuate postprandial blood glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low (~1.8g/100g), MUFA-dominant fat profile from peanut butter (~3.9g MUFA/100g) is cardioprotective, dietary fibre at 5.9g/100g supports LDL reduction, and cholesterol is 0mg/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This food has a favourable fat profile for cardiovascular health; however, sodium at 334mg/100g warrants attention — individuals with CVD should select no-added-salt peanut butter and wholemeal raisin bread to maximise fibre benefit and minimise sodium contribution.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: MUFA-dominant fat from peanut butter (~3.9g/100g) and low saturated fat (~1.8g/100g) support an anti-inflammatory fat profile; fibre at 5.9g/100g supports gut microbiome immune modulation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Choose natural peanut butter without added palm oil or hydrogenated fats to preserve the anti-inflammatory fat profile, and select wholemeal raisin bread to reduce glycaemic load and increase fibre for gut health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Raisin bread with peanut butter is a composite bread-based dish consisting of sliced raisin bread — a lightly sweetened, soft-crumbed bread enriched with dried raisins — spread with peanut butter. The raisin bread provides a mildly sweet, chewy base with a tender crumb, while the peanut butter contributes a rich, creamy, nutty flavour and a smooth or crunchy texture depending on the variety used. This combination is commonly consumed as a breakfast item or snack in Singapore and across Western-influenced food cultures. Nutritionally, the dish provides 315 kcal per 100g, with a moderate protein content of 11.4g from the peanut butter, a moderate fat content of 9.0g dominated by heart-healthy monounsaturated fats (~3.9g MUFA), and a high carbohydrate content of 47.9g per 100g. Dietary fibre is notable at 5.9g/100g, supporting digestive health and partially moderating the glycaemic response. Sodium is 334mg/100g, which is borderline-high and warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. Calcium is high at 227mg/100g, likely reflecting fortification of the bread. The estimated glycaemic index of 70 (high) means this food raises blood glucose relatively quickly, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The peanut butter component contributes vitamin E, polyphenols, and a favourable unsaturated fat profile that supports cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory health. This food is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is free of dairy and cholesterol, but contains gluten and peanuts as major allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id F05020099, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, MUFA, and PUFA estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite data for raisin bread and peanut butter components, clearly documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for raisin bread with documented estimation basis. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.8g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.9g/100g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.8g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for raisin bread (USDA #18027) and smooth peanut butter (USDA #16098) in approximate proportions consistent with the HPB composite macros; HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this item. 2. Trans fat reported as 0.0g/100g based on USDA data confirming negligible trans fat in standard raisin bread and natural peanut butter. 3. Vitamin and mineral fields set to -1 (data not available) for: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — these micronutrient fractions are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this composite item, and USDA component-level data was not used to fill these fields to avoid compounding estimation uncertainty. 4. Potassium (70mg/100g), calcium (227mg/100g), iron (3.8mg/100g), and cholesterol (0.0mg/100g) are preserved from the HPB canonical record. 5. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database raisin bread entries; no direct GI measurement for this exact composite exists in the approved GI database. 6. Cholesterol is 0.0mg/100g — confirmed zero per HPB source; this food contains no animal-derived fat components that would contribute dietary cholesterol. 7. Sugar at 1.16g/100g is notably low for a raisin bread product; this value is preserved from the HPB canonical record and may reflect the specific formulation tested. 8. Calcium at 227mg/100g is high and likely reflects bread fortification; this value is preserved from HPB without adjustment.
